title: Episode 2 — Iran War Justification (v11)
description: The Foundry Council debates whether the United States was justified in joining Israel's preventive war on Iran. Mercer argues yes — a regime weeks from breakout, with the inspectors locked out, cannot be allowed the courtesy of legal patience. Tanaka argues no — five of the six tests of just war fail on the documentary record; preventive is not preemption. Kurogane is the Swing — and finds his floor in a hotel in Muscat where an American diplomat was still talking when the bombs fell. Re-rendered on v11 doctrine: listener-as-juror frame, no side taken by the show, jargon translated in-context, oration toolchest deployed across categories, simplified verdict (three votes + tiebreaker). v10 version archived for diff/learning purposes.
summary: Was the United States justified in joining Israel's preventive war on Iran? Tanaka, Kurogane, and Mercer debate jus ad bellum, the Muscat track, and imminence-by-capability. Juliet moderates. v11 doctrine.
